155793.explanation: The Netherlands has a representative democracy: residents choose parties to represent them in the municipal council and make decisions. The SGP-Christian Union supports this system. Referenda give the illusion of influence and pretend that complex issues can be encapsulated in a single simple question.

155794.explanation: The municipality and government must be easily accessible to everyone, including residents who lack digital skills. Therefore, there must always be a physical location where residents can get help. In our village, that is Het Bonkelaarhuis, where you can go with questions.

155795.explanation: The right forms of participation that help make plans better suited to the village are very valuable. But meetings should be organized only when there are really concrete plans; if residents want to engage, they can also attend council meetings or contact council members.

155796.explanation: Sliedrecht is a village with its own character, appearance, and history. The same applies to the surrounding villages. It is important to preserve that and pay attention to everyone’s individuality. We prefer to work intensively with these and other Drechtsteden rather than merging.

155797.explanation: Sliedrecht is too small to manage everything by itself. Cooperation with other municipalities is necessary to remain independent. It is important, however, that attention is paid to Sliedrecht’s residents and situation. The Social Service Drechtsteden is a good example of this.

155798.explanation: Smart Delta Drechtsteden is a great collaboration between companies, schools, municipalities, and residents to live, work, and enjoy life comfortably in the region. In recent years, this has brought millions of euros to our residents. Unnecessary costs and administrative burdens must be avoided.

155799.explanation: The principle of the SGP-ChristenUnie is: the polluter or the user pays. This also applies to dog owners. Dogs use public spaces and sometimes leave something behind. To keep our municipality clean, healthy, and pleasant, everyone must contribute.

155800.explanation: Currently, the property tax (ozb) in Sliedrecht is lower than the national average. This is attractive for entrepreneurs and that is good. However, we find it reasonable that the contribution from entrepreneurs should grow along with developments at the national level.

155801.explanation: With its location on the Beneden Merwede and a rich dredging history, Sliedrecht is a place where water plays an important role. To increase the attractiveness of our harbor, options such as more moorings for recreational boating are being considered without compromising commercial shipping.

155802.explanation: It is important that refugees are involved in society from the start and can actively contribute. The sooner people understand the Dutch language and can participate in society, the sooner they feel part of the Sliedrecht community.

155803.explanation: Currently, national legislation is being developed in which so-called status holders (refugees with a residence permit) are not included in the list of priority groups. Nevertheless, it is important that the municipality pays attention to housing for these people.

155805.explanation: Waste separation must be encouraged. Pay-as-you-throw (Diftar) is an option, but only if communication to residents about the necessity of waste separation does not have the desired effect. This requires an annual evaluation.

155806.explanation: Local residents are closely involved in plans for sustainable energy generation and can share in the benefits. This can be done by becoming co-owners of a sustainable form of energy generation and sharing the (financial) proceeds.

155807.explanation: In Sliedrecht, there must be enough space for everyone to park and charge near their home; this also applies if you have an electric vehicle. For this, the current charging points must first be used optimally and options such as charging stations in streetlights should be considered.

155808.explanation: The principle that the polluter pays certainly applies in this case. There is only one party responsible for the pollution and thus also for the costs of removing this pollution, and that is Chemours. Landowners must hold Chemours liable.

155809.explanation: This is already happening through Stichting Leergeld and will continue to do so in our view. In addition, our motion 'Sliedrechters Debt-Free and Active' has recently enabled significant funding to ensure poverty is not a barrier to participation in Sliedrecht.

155810.explanation: It is important that residents of Sliedrecht can be active in and around Sliedrecht; this includes swimming. A swimming pool is crucial to facilitate this. Together with Hardinxveld-Giessendam, we are able to realize a swimming pool with an outdoor pool and good facilities, something that would not be possible alone.

155811.explanation: Both municipalities alone can only build a small pool for swimming lessons and swimming clubs. By cooperating, we can provide our residents with a full-fledged swimming pool with an outdoor pool at the same or lower cost. The alternative is that the pools become competitors.

155812.explanation: Everyone must be safe in Sliedrecht. In some places in Sliedrecht, people sometimes do not feel safe due to nuisance; think of the Kerkbuurt or the Burgemeester Winkelerplein. Therefore, besides extra deployment of enforcement officers (BOAs), the use of cameras and public space management must also be considered.

155813.explanation: Everyone must be safe in Sliedrecht. In some places in Sliedrecht, people sometimes do not feel safe due to nuisance; think of the Kerkbuurt or the Burgemeester Winkelerplein. Therefore, besides extra deployment of enforcement officers (BOAs), the use of cameras must also be considered.

155814.explanation: A healthy Sliedrecht for every resident. That is what the SGP-ChristenUnie stands for. This also means actively enforcing alcohol and drug policies to reduce health damage and nuisance. In our view, this also includes closing the coffeeshop.

155816.explanation: In new housing projects, residents of Sliedrecht are carefully given priority so that moving activities within the village increase and more suitable homes become available for both young families and residents looking to move within the community.

155817.explanation: Due to the rapid aging of the Netherlands, it is important to pay attention to shortages that arise in some sectors. In new housing projects, it should be considered whether there are enough homes available for the working population so they can also contribute to the region.

155818.explanation: Nationally, the standard is 60% affordable owner-occupied housing and 30% social rental housing. There must be a good mix of social rental and (affordable) owner-occupied homes to create livable neighborhoods. Sliedrecht is already well above 30%. This means not every plan needs to meet these quantities.

155819.explanation: In building new homes, the demand must be met and future developments for Sliedrecht must be considered. Based on that, the required number of rental and owner-occupied homes must be determined.

155820.explanation: Public housing is a task we want to continue to address regionally. However, this means all municipalities must ensure at least 30% social rental housing is provided. Currently, there is an uneven distribution with Sliedrecht well above 30%.

155821.explanation: Sliedrecht must be a place where everyone can live comfortably in safe, green, and livable neighborhoods. Therefore, it is important to focus on preserving greenery when building within the city and to look at how more greenery can be added in existing neighborhoods during redesign.

155823.explanation: All residents can go to Het Bonkelaarhuis with financial questions. Help is offered to everyone. We must not wait until someone lives below the minimum level. Regarding minimum provisions (financial compensation), there is no reason to raise the income threshold now.

155824.explanation: Those who care for others should not suffer burdensome obstacles themselves. Therefore, barriers must be removed for everyone who is a caregiver. If someone spends a lot of time on caregiving and therefore cannot work and lives on benefits, there is no need to require mandatory job applications.

155826.explanation: In developing the North-South connection, it is good to look at how to create a green and pleasant living environment. It is also possible to include further development of exercise- and sports-friendly public spaces.

155827.explanation: Everyone must adhere to the maximum speed limit and traffic rules in Sliedrecht; this ensures safety in our village and prevents accidents. In certain places, it is necessary to maintain speed bumps to prevent speeding. These must therefore remain.

155828.explanation: Everyone wants to be able to move smoothly and safely in Sliedrecht. When developing Sliedrecht-North, careful consideration was given to how to best structure access to the Sliedrecht center. For this, the North-South connection is crucial.

155830.explanation: Currently, the SGP-ChristenUnie prefers to first look at other necessary connections that need to be made. Think of a good connection between Sliedrecht and the Albert Schweitzer hospital.

155831.explanation: The choice of whether or not to give priority to cyclists at roundabouts should mainly be determined by traffic safety. So far, research has not shown that traffic safety improves when cyclists have priority at roundabouts. This must therefore be researched first.

155832.explanation: There must be continued emphasis on separated and well-lit bicycle paths along the main road structure. This includes, among others, Deltalaan, Merwestraat, Rambrandtlaan, etc.

155833.explanation: There must be continuous attention to freedom of choice in necessary appropriate and/or identity-based care. For the SGP-ChristenUnie, it is paramount that everyone receives care that fits their faith, culture, or beliefs. This also applies in youth care.

155834.explanation: Het Bonkelaarhuis has proven in recent years to be an important link in care and support in Sliedrecht. It is highly appreciated by residents and others. This must remain so, and therefore, no budget cuts should be made to Het Bonkelaarhuis.

155835.explanation: Currently, there is a plan initiated by the SGP-ChristenUnie to experiment with a low-threshold relationship point in Het Bonkelaarhuis and to cover its costs. We focus on healthy relationships, regardless of whether children are involved. Healthy relationships have a positive effect on everyone.

208447.explanation: In Sliedrecht, genuine refugees are accommodated at least as long as there is a local assignment based on the applicable Distribution Act. People in need who flee to our country can count on compassionate policy. The construction of a new asylum seekers' center can only take place if the set conditions are met.

155793.explanation: We want residents to have the opportunity to give their opinion on topics that were not discussed during the campaign, such as the demolition of the Lockhorst.

155794.explanation: In the center of Sliedrecht we have Het Bonkelaarhuis. We have not received any signals from the village that anything like this is needed in the East or West.

155795.explanation: As far as PRO Sliedrecht is concerned, this may take place prior to every municipal council meeting.

155796.explanation: Sliedrecht is and remains independent in our opinion.

155797.explanation: We do locally what can be done locally. We do regionally what must be done.

155798.explanation: This cooperation must demonstrably deliver results for Sliedrecht. At that moment, we have no objections to investing money.

155799.explanation: A dog serves a social function for many of our residents.

155800.explanation: The property tax (ozb) has been raised too often in recent years without residents getting anything in return.

155801.explanation: In our view, the harbor may have a much greater recreational function. This may partly come at the expense of moorings for professional shipping.

155802.explanation: These costs lie with the national government. The municipality has no control over this.

155803.explanation: We adhere to the number allocated to us by the national government.

155805.explanation: The polluter pays. Proper waste separation must be rewarded. Medical waste is excluded from this.

155806.explanation: There are sufficient arrangements for sustainability in the municipality of Sliedrecht. Help does not always need to be financial but can also be in the form of support.

155807.explanation: We support having enough charging stations in the village, but these parking spaces may be used by everyone.

155808.explanation: Developments in Sliedrecht must not come to a standstill. The municipality will recover the costs from Chemours through legal means. The polluter pays.

155809.explanation: The municipality of Sliedrecht will always ensure these people are supported.

155810.explanation: We believe there should be a swimming pool, including an outdoor pool, at the current location.

155811.explanation: The many millions that this project costs could be better invested in Lockhorst.

155812.explanation: As long as these people are deployed for issues that residents care about. An example of this is parking nuisance.

155813.explanation: Installing cameras shifts the problem. We would rather see the underlying problem solved.

155814.explanation: We are against drug use but prefer that it is sold in a regulated way rather than criminals profiting from it.

155816.explanation: We have been committed to this for years. We have succeeded in arranging this for owner-occupied homes. Now for rental homes.

155817.explanation: In our view, this could be implemented even more broadly.

155818.explanation: We adhere to the standard of 30% social rental housing as it is currently nationally. If this is increased, we have no objections.

155820.explanation: In our opinion, moving away is not necessary. However, we would like to explore how youth from Sliedrecht can get housing in the village faster.

155821.explanation: In our view, rebuilding should only be done on existing building locations.

155823.explanation: We believe that supportive measures should apply to the standard of 130% of the statutory social minimum.

155824.explanation: If people help their loved ones, they should not be obliged to look for work.

155826.explanation: In our view, a green strip is not necessary to build a functional bridge.

155827.explanation: Every road in Sliedrecht may be designated as a 30-kilometer zone, in our opinion.

155828.explanation: This connection is linked to building in the Green Heart. We are against that.

155830.explanation: If sufficient demand arises, we have nothing against a night bus. We are not aware of such a wish at this moment.

155831.explanation: The priority regulation should be aligned with that of neighboring municipalities. This prevents confusion.

155832.explanation: This decision has already been made.

155833.explanation: People should be given this freedom.

155834.explanation: The Bonkelaarhuis is highly valued by our residents.

155835.explanation: We must reimburse it, but not force families to do so.

208447.explanation: As long as the dispersion law exists, the municipality must do this to prevent the province from obliging the municipality of Sliedrecht to accommodate a larger number of asylum seekers in our village for an extended period.

155793.explanation: Residents of Sliedrecht are represented by the members of the municipal council. For discussing important issues, these members can be approached by residents. Moreover, the CDA does not believe in a 'winner takes it all' mentality.

155794.explanation: The combination of central locations (Bonkelaarhuis and municipal office), mobile guidance, and digital support is sufficient for the size of Sliedrecht.

155795.explanation: Democracy Day (September 15) is a good date for such meetings. It is good to celebrate local democracy and bring it as close to residents as possible through meetings.

155796.explanation: A governmental merger with neighboring municipality(ies) currently offers no added value; the local ambition level of CDA Sliedrecht is too high for that.

155797.explanation: Of course, Sliedrecht cannot tackle all challenges alone; regional cooperation when necessary or efficient, local cooperation when possible or effective.

155798.explanation: CDA Sliedrecht wants to organize a stronger network for practical education between schools, healthcare institutions, and companies in the region. Spending less money on SDD means spending less money on the future viability of Sliedrecht's economic engines (healthcare and maritime technology).

155799.explanation: Dog tax is not sacred, but abolition leads to an increase in other municipal taxes.

155800.explanation: If municipal taxes need to be increased, it is preferable to raise taxes on usage rather than on ownership.

155801.explanation: Expansion of the marina is at most a desire, but there is no necessity.

155802.explanation: Language is an important element of our culture and therefore crucial for the integration of new Dutch residents. By linking language to participation, such as volunteer work and social activities, newcomers get to know our society faster and participate actively sooner.

155803.explanation: We believe everyone has the right to housing; including people going through an asylum procedure. Additionally, there are other target groups (singles, youth, etc.) who find it difficult to find a home. For them, this becomes challenging if priority rules apply. Because Sliedrecht hosts asylum seekers from Alblasserdam municipality in the AZC, Alblasserdam accommodates status holders for us. That is a good agreement.

155805.explanation: Sliedrecht deserves a future-proof and innovative waste policy, where the flexibilization of tariffs for households is open for discussion.

155806.explanation: The Energy Act 2026 enables sharing locally generated energy within energy communities. The CDA wants to seize this opportunity for new construction, for example in Sliedrecht-North, and also make this form of sharing financially feasible.

155807.explanation: The supply must grow in line with demand, also in public spaces.

155808.explanation: Soil pollution is a societal problem, not just a Sliedrecht problem. Each case should be assessed to determine how excavation can be financially enabled.

155809.explanation: Money should not be a barrier to joining a sports club.

155810.explanation: A new swimming pool with a regional function should be made possible near the municipal border, which Sliedrecht residents can use.

155811.explanation: A new swimming pool with a regional function should be made possible near the municipal border, which Sliedrecht residents and residents of other municipalities can use. Cooperation with Hardinxveld-Giessendam is a logical option.

155812.explanation: As the availability of police officers for enforcement tasks decreases, enforcement officers (BOAs) are the best alternative.

155813.explanation: At locations where nuisance or criminal behavior occurs regularly, temporary camera surveillance adds value. However, camera surveillance does not provide a structural solution, especially not at a location like Winklerplein.

155814.explanation: CDA Sliedrecht does not see the current coffeeshop on A.W. de Landgraafstraat as an asset to the village, but as a compromise. For now, there is no reason to close this location as soon as possible.

155816.explanation: Following the national Housing Act, a portion of new-build or rental homes may be reserved for local residents or specific groups, such as teachers, police officers, and healthcare workers. CDA Sliedrecht considers this accommodating.

155817.explanation: Following the national Housing Act, a portion of new-build or rental homes may be reserved for local residents or specific groups, such as teachers, police officers, and healthcare workers. CDA Sliedrecht considers this accommodating.

155818.explanation: Regionally, Sliedrecht does not have a shortage of social rental homes or homes with a healthcare function, so an above-average increase in new social rental homes should not be a priority.

155819.explanation: At least two-thirds of the homes should be in an affordable segment.

155820.explanation: As long as Woonkeus Drechtsteden meets the implementation of Sliedrecht policy in regional allocation in the coming years, CDA Sliedrecht sees no reason to detach Sliedrecht from this platform.

155821.explanation: Of course, green spaces are very valuable, but also the rapid realization of new homes has a high priority. For each project, it must be balanced how as much green as possible can be preserved or compensated near the new homes; green and housing development must strengthen each other.

155823.explanation: Currently, CDA Sliedrecht sees no reason to question the current boundary.

155824.explanation: The obligation to apply for work and the extension of informal caregiving do not conflict with each other.

155826.explanation: In accordance with current planning, greening the surroundings of this infrastructure has great added value.

155827.explanation: Speed bumps are an effective means to ensure the vast majority of road users drive at the desired speed. If it is determined that vibration nuisance is excessive, a better alternative must be sought.

155828.explanation: CDA Sliedrecht continues to advocate for a new north-south connection. This connection can relieve and downgrade Stationsweg to 30 km/h, make Sliedrecht-North a full-fledged part of Sliedrecht, add greenery, and greatly improve access to the station and sports clubs.

155830.explanation: Occasionally partying in Rotterdam can be fun, but it is not a task of the Municipality of Sliedrecht. People who drive by car to Rotterdam for a party are expected to remain fit and not consume alcohol.

155831.explanation: Sliedrecht has positive figures regarding traffic safety, and the number of accidents at roundabouts remains controlled. It is very important that this remains the case. Also, there is no room around current roundabouts to redesign them into safe roundabouts with cyclists having priority.

155832.explanation: CDA Sliedrecht strongly supports new investments in cycling infrastructure, especially along main roads within built-up areas where the speed limit is 50 km/h. Funds must be reserved quickly for this.

155833.explanation: CDA Sliedrecht does not see it as a municipal task to actively pursue this.

155834.explanation: The great added value of the Bonkelaarhuis must be preserved at all costs and may incur expenses.

155835.explanation: CDA Sliedrecht does not see it as a municipal task to actively pursue this.

208447.explanation: CDA Sliedrecht supports the Distribution Act and does not want to shirk the associated responsibilities. CDA Sliedrecht considers the arrival of an AZC combined with a low flow of status holders to Sliedrecht social rental homes a noble decision.

155794.explanation: It depends on how you define 'neighborhood' and how large it is. However, we do agree with organizing sufficient support points to help residents with this.

155795.explanation: Moreover, there are plenty of other ways to engage in dialogue with each other, such as at participation meetings.

155796.explanation: These four municipalities can operate independently very well.

155797.explanation: The current balance between cooperation and self-management is quite adequate.

155798.explanation: Smart Delta Drechtsteden offers various advantages that, although perhaps not directly quantifiable, also strengthen Sliedrecht's position vis-à-vis other governments.

155799.explanation: But there must be a structural coverage for the budget deficit that arises as a result.

155800.explanation: Unless this is balanced by the (retention of) social facilities, or unless this prevents cuts in the social domain.

155801.explanation: No opinion; having and maintaining a marina is not a core municipal task. Permission can be granted as long as the intended expansion does not cost the community money.

155802.explanation: If necessary. The municipality must in any case allocate sufficient funds for this.

155803.explanation: This does not solve the shortage of social rental housing. Moreover, a person with a residence permit is not a regular starter on the housing market; technically, they are homeless. Therefore, there is no level playing field compared to other housing seekers.

155805.explanation: According to the principle of 'the polluter pays.' However, a well-functioning and fair system must be established for this.

155806.explanation: Collective initiatives in this area should be encouraged.

155807.explanation: The number of charging stations should be in balance with the actual demand for them and the parking needs of non-electric cars.

155808.explanation: Basically agree. At least for our own projects and works in public spaces.

155809.explanation: The municipality must be willing to spend money on this, particularly to counteract lack of physical activity early on. The Sliedrecht club life is also very positive about this.

155810.explanation: Sliedrecht must, in any case, have a fully-fledged swimming pool that meets the needs of all users—so for associations, recreational users, and swimming lessons.

155811.explanation: Given the current status of a joint swimming pool and the expected financial and procedural obstacles, this is an undesirable development.

155812.explanation: We prefer to see more mobile youth workers on the streets to proactively prevent undesirable behavior and the resulting feeling of insecurity.

155813.explanation: We are very cautious about the use of camera surveillance unless there is an urgent necessity.

155814.explanation: The coffeeshop does not cause nuisance and has taken excellent organizational measures on this point. Closing the coffeeshop would inevitably lead to street dealing and its related implications.

155816.explanation: As far as possible within existing laws and regulations.

155817.explanation: We have not yet formed a position on this.

155818.explanation: Although approximately 37% of housing in Sliedrecht is social rental housing at the moment, we strongly agree with this statement.

155819.explanation: In the housing differentiation we desire—social rental/mid-rent/affordable owner-occupied—there remains a maximum of 30% space for other categories such as more expensive owner-occupied.

155820.explanation: At the moment, we cannot foresee what the side effects of this will be.

155823.explanation: In Sliedrecht, the income limit is already relatively high, namely at 140%. It should mainly stay that way.

155824.explanation: In this, we follow the line of the national party concerning the relaxation of rules for caregivers receiving social assistance.

155826.explanation: There must be a proper connection to Sliedrecht North that fits within the municipality’s financial possibilities.

155827.explanation: Our goal is to make the entire village center a 30-kilometer zone, including the associated layout.

155828.explanation: Our position on this will strongly depend on the affordability of such a connection.

155830.explanation: We are not aware of the extent of the demand for this.

155831.explanation: Just like in all other Drechtsteden municipalities.

155832.explanation: Unless the Deltalaan is also made a 30-kilometer zone.

155833.explanation: Can be seen in the context of previously adopted positions on appropriate daytime activities and suitable transportation.

155834.explanation: There are no cuts to the social domain.

155835.explanation: We stand for affordable care and support for families. However, the municipality's contribution should be determined based on income.

208447.explanation: Sliedrecht helps in solving the problems in Ter Apel and Budel.

155793.explanation: Slydregt.NU wants to introduce a referendum ordinance following the model of the Association of Dutch Municipalities (VNG), enabling both the community and the municipal council to initiate a referendum.

155794.explanation: Slydregt.NU believes the municipality should be more visible in helping residents. We see the municipal office and Het Bonkelaarhuis as good places where residents can get help filling out forms.

155795.explanation: Slydregt.NU wants our residents, entrepreneurs, educational institutions, associations, and other interested parties to have more opportunities to ask questions and present initiatives.

155796.explanation: Sliedrecht is a village with a unique character. This is evident in how Sliedrechters meet and live together. Slydregt.NU wants to preserve this connectedness. Therefore, we oppose any future merger to ensure Sliedrecht maintains its own identity and close-knit community.

155797.explanation: Slydregt.NU wants the municipality of Sliedrecht to focus on its core tasks and only cooperate where necessary, such as with the environmental service, safety region, and Rotterdam police unit. We aim for more control and connection in our regional collaborations.

155798.explanation: Slydregt.NU wants the municipality of Sliedrecht to focus on its core tasks and only cooperate where necessary, such as with the environmental service, safety region, and Rotterdam police unit. We aim for more control and connection in our regional collaborations.

155799.explanation: Slydregt.NU wants to handle the Sliedrechter’s finances wisely. We want transparency about the choices made in the tax ordinance. Therefore, we want to revise the current tax ordinance and abolish unnecessary taxes, such as the dog tax.

155800.explanation: At Slydregt.NU, we advocate transparency regarding the Sliedrechter’s finances. The municipal administration must be honest about why certain choices are made concerning taxes. We oppose structurally increasing municipal taxes annually, such as the property tax (OZB).

155801.explanation: Slydregt.NU wants more space for presenting initiatives from society. If an initiative to expand the Sliedrecht marina falls within legal frameworks, we can approve it.

155802.explanation: Slydregt.NU wants to preserve our culture. This means everything that makes Sliedrecht great. Investments, facilitation, and advice are needed without interference from the municipal administration. This way, traditions and values that make Sliedrecht unique will be maintained.

155803.explanation: In allocating social rental homes, people with residence permits are given priority, causing Sliedrecht youth, singles, and the elderly to be left behind. Slydregt.NU chooses a course where homes remain available to Sliedrechters who shape our village.

155805.explanation: Slydregt.NU wants fewer rules and bureaucracy so the municipal government can return to its core tasks. Paying extra for more residual waste does not fit this approach. We choose clarity and fairness for our residents.

155806.explanation: Slydregt.NU wants to work on a realistic energy transition that has public support. We prioritize the free choice of the people of Sliedrecht. We choose a different approach, facilitating society to take its own responsibility.

155807.explanation: Slydregt.NU sees heavy parking pressure and parking standards that are too low in Sliedrecht. While parking spaces are disappearing, our residents need more space to park. We choose to preserve and expand parking facilities.

155808.explanation: Slydregt.NU advocates for stricter enforcement at Chemours and wants to lobby higher government levels, such as the province, the national government, water boards, and the European Union. PFAS pollution must stop. We must work towards: 'Zero emissions from the pipe!'

155809.explanation: Slydregt.NU believes the municipality should better support sports clubs so that sports are accessible to all Sliedrecht residents.

155810.explanation: Slydregt.NU believes Sliedrecht must always have a swimming pool within its territory. We support preserving De Lockhorst, including its swimming pool (with outdoor pool), event center, and sports hall.

155811.explanation: Slydregt.NU believes Sliedrecht must always have a swimming pool within its territory. We support preserving De Lockhorst, including its swimming pool (with outdoor pool), event center, and sports hall.

155812.explanation: Slydregt.NU wants more enforcement through a local police plan and clear prioritization. Residents must feel that reporting is effective. Therefore, prioritization and the role of enforcers and community wardens must be part of a robust safety plan.

155813.explanation: Slydregt.NU wants to tackle serious disturbances and incidents by deploying surveillance cameras, prohibiting gatherings, and pursuing damage claims. For youth nuisance, we believe it is important to invest in the neighborhood and in youth work by SOJS.

155814.explanation: Slydregt.NU wants to facilitate entrepreneurs, not restrict them. We will stop coercion and pressure from the municipal government and choose cooperation with residents, entrepreneurs, educational institutions, and associations to strengthen Sliedrecht.

155816.explanation: Slydregt.NU wants a different course and opts for a housing supply for Sliedrecht residents. We mean residents who are economically and socially connected to our village. These are people who do not let Sliedrecht deteriorate but help shape it.

155817.explanation: Slydregt.NU wants a different course and opts for a housing supply for Sliedrecht residents. We mean residents who are economically and socially connected to our village. These are people who do not let Sliedrecht deteriorate but help shape it.

155818.explanation: In 2024, 34% of homes in Sliedrecht were rental properties from housing corporations, and 8% were other rental homes. Slydregt.NU believes there is a limit to our social provisions in Sliedrecht. Therefore, we prioritize our young starters who want to buy a house in Sliedrecht.

155819.explanation: Slydregt.NU is concerned about the housing issue. We see that, partly because of a too-large migration balance, demand in the housing market is high. This means we need to build new homes. Slydregt.NU works with three conditions: own postal code first, feasible, and affordable.

155820.explanation: Slydregt.NU sees Woonkeus as a burden. Anyone in the Netherlands can register with Woonkeus. This creates too much pressure on Sliedrecht’s housing market and means Sliedrecht has no control over its own homes.

155821.explanation: Slydregt.NU sees densification plans as a threat to our livability and safety. We want to work with our community for a greener Sliedrecht, with more balanced flora and fauna.

155823.explanation: Slydregt.NU wants poverty to be tackled. Raising the income threshold is not the right measure for us. We prefer a broader approach by cooperating with engaged residents, entrepreneurs, educational institutions, and associations.

155824.explanation: Slydregt.NU finds this measure unfeasible. We focus on fewer rules, less bureaucracy, and deregulation, allowing the municipal government to return to its core tasks.

155826.explanation: Slydregt.NU opposes the current plans for an expensive North-South connection. We prefer to look at effective alternatives to keep traffic flowing smoothly in Sliedrecht.

155827.explanation: Slydregt.NU wants cars to move through the village smoothly and safely. These traffic obstacles cause annoyance and make smooth flow more difficult. We fight against unnecessary and unsafe traffic obstacles but choose enforcement.

155828.explanation: Slydregt.NU opposes the current plans for an expensive North-South connection. We prefer to look at effective alternatives to keep traffic flowing smoothly in Sliedrecht.

155830.explanation: Slydregt.NU wants the night bus to return. Many Sliedrecht youths study in big cities and need to get home safely. With the night bus, we give them the chance to fully enjoy student life.

155831.explanation: For Slydregt.NU, it is important that all roundabouts within municipal boundaries have the same priority rules.

155832.explanation: For Slydregt.NU, it is important that projects in Sliedrecht are completed before measures like these are taken.

155833.explanation: Slydregt.NU wants to preserve our culture. That means everything that makes Sliedrecht unique. To protect our culture, we believe helpers and caregivers must offer their help and care neutrally.

155834.explanation: Slydregt.NU wants to cherish important Sliedrecht facilities and invest in cooperation with Het Bonkelaarhuis.

155835.explanation: Slydregt.NU wants to end the municipal government’s coercion and pressure towards residents, entrepreneurs, educational institutions, and associations, and instead facilitate cooperation and society. We want the municipality to return to its core tasks.

208447.explanation: Slydregt.NU wants to stop preparations for opening a refugee reception center (AZC) in 2027 as soon as possible. We oppose the arrival of an AZC in Sliedrecht. The pressure on our safety is too great, and our caring community is at its limit.

155793.explanation: The municipal council represents the residents. A referendum suggests it reflects the entire community’s opinion. Only those who vote are consulted. A 90% turnout might make it representative, but that has never happened in an election.

155794.explanation: That will cost residents a lot of money! The Sliedrecht VVD understands that some need help with forms. That help can be provided at one place in Sliedrecht. Sliedrecht is small enough that everything is reachable by car, bike, or on foot. Also, the internet is helpful for residents.

155795.explanation: This is unnecessary. At the Sliedrecht VVD, it is possible to contact council members every day. The municipal council meets regularly, allowing residents to meet council members and speak at council sessions.

155796.explanation: A merger with another municipality cannot be ruled out in the long term. Whether this should be with all municipalities is a big step. Sliedrecht is too small as a municipality to continue offering all specialist services well. Through cooperation and merger, it is possible.

155797.explanation: Sliedrecht is too small to organize everything itself for housing, economy, innovation, and accessibility. The national and provincial governments subsidize regions that cooperate. Cooperation with other municipalities is therefore necessary!

155798.explanation: The national and provincial governments subsidize regions like the Drechtsteden. Residents and companies work across municipal or national borders. This is a good thing. More than half of the residents work in the Drechtsteden. The Drechtsteden have secured a lot of funding, including for Sliedrecht.

155799.explanation: As long as the dog tax is part of the structural budget and a lot of money is spent on dog walking areas and poop bags, it must come from somewhere. That means other taxes would have to increase. The Sliedrecht VVD wants to abolish it if cuts are made.

155800.explanation: Costs for residents and businesses are increasing disproportionately. This is because we provide some services that are not legally required. We should look into stopping some services to save money. We can be more critical about that.

155801.explanation: The Sliedrecht VVD believes the economy is more important. That means the municipal port must remain suitable for that. Expansion of the marina is not an option. The long waiting lists for members should be addressed by the association itself.

155802.explanation: It is good that money is invested in the integration of people who do not yet master the language. Language is an important link to connect and get to know each other. Spending more money on refugees is absolutely not necessary.

155803.explanation: This is not a big problem. The problem is the broad housing supply, which causes too little turnover. The waiting time for a rental home is not due to status holders but because too few people move. Giving priority is therefore not an option as long as too few homes are built.

155805.explanation: The Sliedrecht VVD applies the principle 'the polluter pays.' The core is that good waste separation leads to lower costs. So, the answer should be yes. The question is whether it is necessary. Technology separates cheaper and better than people. Centralized waste separation is best.

155806.explanation: Sustainable energy production is not done with public money. If production is not profitable, you should not start. The investment must pay for itself.

155807.explanation: The Sliedrecht VVD wants all parking spaces to be equipped with charging points. Then there will be no distinction between those who charge and those who do not. In addition, more and more cars will be electric, increasing demand for charging. Therefore, the distinction must disappear.

155808.explanation: The Sliedrecht VVD follows the principle 'the polluter pays.' Chemours must clean up the PFAS pollution. They are actually doing this for vegetable gardens and want to cooperate with the municipality to clean playgrounds and similar areas.

155809.explanation: The Sliedrecht VVD believes everyone should be able to play sports or exercise. That can be done in different ways. For parents with a limited budget, there are options to let their child play sports. It is also about making choices, however difficult that sometimes is. So, not automatically paying for membership.

155810.explanation: It is great that neighbors are exploring the idea of a shared swimming pool together. The choice to go swimming is not about having a pool nearby, but about what suits you and what you feel like doing. For example, 50% of residents in surrounding municipalities obtain a swimming diploma.

155811.explanation: That is the best option! A shared swimming pool offers more than having your own pool. The choice to go swimming is not about having a pool nearby, but about what suits you and what you feel like doing. For example, 50% of residents in surrounding municipalities obtain a swimming diploma. Together, you can offer more for less cost.

155812.explanation: According to the Sliedrecht VVD, extra funds were recently allocated for enforcement. If that proves insufficient, it can be expanded with proper justification. Of course, it is regrettable that so much enforcement is necessary. All residents are involved in this.

155813.explanation: The Sliedrecht VVD has long believed that camera surveillance contributes to safety. So also for safety at Winklerplein. This may also be further expanded if needed for security.

155814.explanation: The Sliedrecht VVD sees no reason to close the coffee shop. Drug nuisance and dealers must be tackled. That is not caused by the coffee shop in Sliedrecht.

155816.explanation: Priority is about urgency. More homes need to be built. That helps improve flow. The term 'housing seeker' is broad. Someone with a child in a small house is just as much a housing seeker as a starter or a senior looking to move.

155817.explanation: If they also work in the Drechtsteden. The Sliedrecht VVD welcomes that condition. If residents then choose these professions, this is a nice incentive!

155818.explanation: Sliedrecht has many social rental homes. As long as Sliedrecht has more than 30% social rental homes, we must aim to reduce to 30%. Social rental homes can be built, but in the ratio of 20% social, 30% affordable, and 50% market-rate homes. No more than 30% social rental in all of Sliedrecht!

155819.explanation: There is a great need for homes in the higher price segment. This also facilitates flow in Sliedrecht of cheaper homes. Too many people have left Sliedrecht because they found an expensive home nearby. At least 40%, preferably 50%.

155820.explanation: The Sliedrecht VVD understands that social rental homes exist but advocates building more owner-occupied homes. There is no pro or con regarding cooperation between housing corporations for allocating homes.

155821.explanation: That does not happen either. By better organizing and building higher in a residential block, more space remains for livability on the street and in the neighborhood, and more green space returns. For Sliedrecht’s growth, building upwards is necessary. The Sliedrecht VVD supports adding one more floor!

155823.explanation: The Sliedrecht VVD believes that the arrangements in Sliedrecht are generous and clearly above the social minimum. It is interesting to see the causes of poverty and to help people learn how to manage money and setbacks.

155824.explanation: We have a huge shortage of working people in Sliedrecht and the Drechtsteden. Our companies are desperate for people. Many working people also care for their loved ones as informal caregivers. Many employers are willing to accommodate this temporarily with flexible arrangements.

155826.explanation: The green lung may come. Within what is affordable, we must ensure investments for the future. Everything has a price. The Sliedrecht VVD is happy to invest in a good and green living environment. If it can be more than a green strip, we should do that!

155827.explanation: On primary roads, we must ensure smooth traffic flow and avoid obstacles or speed bumps. Speed bumps are not always functional. Often, the main traffic offenders live nearby themselves. A guest usually behaves like a guest.

155828.explanation: Sliedrecht already experiences congestion during rush hour. With a new neighborhood Sliedrecht-North added, the consequences are unpredictable. It is therefore necessary that this connection is made and Sliedrecht remains easily accessible.

155830.explanation: The Sliedrecht VVD sees no need to pay for a night bus between Rotterdam and Sliedrecht. Youth manage this well themselves nowadays via the internet. The elderly cope perfectly well with the public transport schedule in Sliedrecht.

155831.explanation: It is clear that there are fewer traffic victims at roundabouts than in surrounding municipalities. In Sliedrecht, cyclists and motorists see each other because priority is often given instead of taken.

155832.explanation: The Sliedrecht VVD finds the current layout of the beginning of Deltalaan fine. Due to the higher speeds of electric bicycles, cyclists feel they need more space. That is well possible there. Mopeds were sent back to the roadway because this (higher speed) is ultimately safer.

155833.explanation: That may be desirable but is certainly not decisive. The Sliedrecht VVD believes care should be deliverable. Sometimes you must adapt to the available offer. It is up to people to pay themselves, and then it is not paid by the community.

155834.explanation: The Sliedrecht VVD believes by definition that everything should be open for discussion. That also applies to all social and care tasks that are given beyond legal requirements.

155835.explanation: The Sliedrecht VVD thinks parents are sensible people. There should be no counter where relationship therapy can be claimed arbitrarily.

208447.explanation: The municipality has the legal obligation from the national and provincial government to shelter refugees. The Sliedrecht VVD stands for solving problems and against shifting responsibility to others. We carefully monitor that promised measures related to the asylum seekers center are implemented as committed.

155793.explanation: D66 is in favor of referendums. Residents of Sliedrecht should have a say in important decisions. Currently, clear rules are missing. The referendum is only advisory and can be ignored. That is not real participation.

155794.explanation: It is important to first establish what defines a neighborhood. For Sliedrecht, a division into three areas is good and clear.

155795.explanation: In the election program of D66 Sliedrecht, we opt for a monthly council consultation hour. This gives residents more opportunities to talk directly with council members.

155796.explanation: Merging does not mean losing identity. Combining municipal organizations is cost-efficient. Residents get more options. The names of villages and neighborhoods can remain as sub-municipalities.

155797.explanation: Sliedrecht now relies too much on Dordrecht as a large neighboring municipality. In a merged municipality, there is more balance. Then less dependency is needed, and Sliedrecht stands stronger in the region.

155798.explanation: As a small municipality, Sliedrecht cannot do without regional cooperation. This way, we protect the interests of schools and businesses and ensure strong facilities and opportunities for everyone.

155799.explanation: It is logical that the polluter pays. The community incurs many costs for dog owners, such as cleaning and maintenance. D66 believes owners should be responsible for that themselves.

155800.explanation: The property tax is not a tap that can be used without limits to finance plans. D66 believes taxes should only be used appropriately and after careful planning.

155801.explanation: It is strange that you have to be almost on the waiting list for a berth at birth. D66 believes the marina can be better expanded in other locations in Sliedrecht so that more people can enjoy water and recreation.

155802.explanation: Fast integration goes better if people learn Dutch well. D66 finds language important for participation in work, school, and neighborhood.

155803.explanation: This subject is decided by the national government. D66 Sliedrecht cannot influence this locally.

155805.explanation: Protecting our environment is important for everyone. Preventing residual waste can be done by making people pay for what they throw away.

155806.explanation: D66 wants the municipality to inform residents about national subsidies. This way, everyone can more easily participate in sustainable energy and environmental projects.

155807.explanation: In some neighborhoods, parking pressure is so high that this is not always possible.

155808.explanation: The municipality’s most important task is to ensure a pleasant and healthy living environment. Contaminated soil does not belong there.

155809.explanation: Sport is important for healthy living. Everyone should have access to sports facilities. That is why we consider this a municipal task.

155810.explanation: Sliedrecht currently has over 26,000 inhabitants and likely 35,000 in the future. It is unthinkable that our municipality does not have its own swimming pool. D66 believes there must be a swimming pool within the municipal boundaries.

155811.explanation: This plan has already cost a lot of money. Stop it immediately. It is better first to look at what it costs to maintain the swimming pool within Sliedrecht.

155812.explanation: The deployment of BOAs (security officers) is not always necessary. This only creates a false sense of security. More important is that the municipality focuses on preventing problems.

155813.explanation: Cameras without real follow-up do not help. That is a false sense of security. The municipality must take action against the nuisance itself.

155814.explanation: The law allows a coffeeshop. Banning it solves nothing. It actually increases illegal trade. D66 wants realistic measures to limit the nuisance.

155816.explanation: There is now a housing allocation system at the regional level. Locally, this can lead to inequality. D66 wants fair and clear allocation.

155817.explanation: Those who perform important tasks for Sliedrecht, such as teachers, nurses, and police officers, must be able to live here.

155818.explanation: Our election program states that 30% of the homes should be social housing and 20% mid-rent. This aligns with national policy.

155819.explanation: To improve the housing market, more owner-occupied homes must be built. This also helps to prevent skewed housing.

155820.explanation: It is a system that works, even though it is not perfect for everyone. At this moment, there is no better alternative.

155821.explanation: Green spaces are important for a pleasant living environment. They must not be sacrificed for housing construction. Sliedrecht North offers enough other locations for new homes.

155823.explanation: Poverty is not only about income. It is mainly about the circumstances in which people live.

155824.explanation: Decisions about this topic are made in The Hague. The municipality has no authority over this.

155826.explanation: A functional viaduct is sufficient and affordable. All extras do not improve it and only cost more money.

155827.explanation: A maximum speed limit of 30 kilometers per hour in Sliedrecht makes it safer for everyone.

155828.explanation: Sliedrecht North must become well accessible. However, one can question whether the currently chosen location is really the best.

155830.explanation: The municipality is not a travel agency. A night bus for work or study is possible, but paying for party or nightlife trips is not the municipality's responsibility.

155831.explanation: Different rules apply in the region, but Sliedrecht is safer. Still, it is difficult to understand that we are an exception in the region.

155832.explanation: Cycling in Sliedrecht must be encouraged. That can only happen if it is safe for everyone.

155833.explanation: We think this responsibility lies with the care institution or care organization, such as home care, neighborhood care, or a nursing home. People cannot always choose who is at their bedside.

155834.explanation: In recent years, much has been invested in the Bonkelaarhuis. Therefore, budget cuts cannot be the goal.

155835.explanation: The municipality should not fully pay for couples therapy. Parents should also contribute a part themselves. The municipality can only help where it is truly needed.

208447.explanation: The fairest solution is that the national government distributes the costs fairly among all municipalities.

statements.title: The municipality should abolish the dog tax.

titleParts.glossary: Tax paid to the municipality for owning one or more dogs.

statements.title: The municipality should fully cover relationship therapy for families with children.

titleParts.glossary: When people try to improve their romantic relationship together with a counselor.

titleParts.text: voor gezinnen met kinderen volledig vergoeden.

statements.title: The municipality should spend more on language classes and volunteer programs for refugees.
Sliedrecht must merge in the future with Alblasserdam, Hardinxveld-Giessendam and Papendrecht.

titleParts.glossary: The merging of two or more municipalities.

statements.title: Building houses within the village must not come at the expense of space for parks.
The municipality must allow expansion of the marina in Sliedrecht.
People receiving welfare benefits who provide informal care to a family member or acquaintance do not have to apply for jobs.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

titleParts.text: die een familielid of bekende verzorgen als

titleParts.glossary: Someone who provides unpaid and often long-term care for sick family members or friends.

statements.title: The municipality should give refugees with a residence permit priority for social rental housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: In the new Sliedrecht-Noord development, at least 40% of the homes must be higher-priced owner-occupied homes.
Residents of Sliedrecht must pay more if they put out more residual waste.

titleParts.text: Inwoners van Sliedrecht moeten meer gaan betalen als ze meer

titleParts.glossary: Waste that remains after paper, plastic and organic waste have been collected separately.

statements.title: When allocating social housing, applicants from the municipality should be given priority.

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: moeten woningzoekenden uit de eigen gemeente voorrang krijgen.

statements.title: Sliedrecht must build a new swimming pool together with Hardinxveld-Giessendam.
Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.theme: Dialogue between residents and municipal council members
statements.title: The municipality must organize at least two meetings a year where council members and residents talk with each other.
The municipality must spend less money on Smart Delta Drechtsteden, a partnership between schools, businesses and municipalities in the Drechtsteden region.
The municipality must pay for sports club memberships for children whose parents or guardians have little money.
Residents of Sliedrecht must be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Sliedrecht moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The municipality must close the coffeeshop in Sliedrecht.

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: Of the newly built homes in Sliedrecht, at least 40% must be social rental housing. In the Netherlands, 28% of homes are currently social rentals.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Sliedrecht moet minimaal 40 procent een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Nederland is nu 28 procent van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: Sliedrecht must have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who focus on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: When allocating social rental housing, teachers, nurses and police officers should be given priority.

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: moeten leraren, verpleegkundigen en politieagenten voorrang krijgen.

statements.title: More charging points for electric cars must be installed, even if ordinary parking spaces are lost as a result.
The municipality must not build a new North–South connection over the railway and the A15.
Sliedrecht must increase camera surveillance at Burgemeester Winklerplein.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: A segregated cycle path must be built along the entire Deltalaan.
People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: Cyclists must be given priority at all roundabouts in Sliedrecht.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
As long as Chemours does not do it, the municipality must pay for the removal of soil contaminated by PFAS.

shootoutStatements.title: The municipality should handle more itself and participate less in partnerships with other municipalities.
The property tax (ozb) should be increased.
The municipality should ensure there is a place in every neighborhood where residents can get help filling in forms.
Sliedrecht should keep its own swimming pool.
Sliedrecht should withdraw from Woonkeus, the system that allocates rental homes in the Drechtsteden.
The municipality should invest in a night bus between Sliedrecht and Rotterdam.
The municipality should remove obstacles that prevent cars from driving at higher speeds, such as speed bumps.

shootoutStatements.theme: Green spaces at the North–South connection
shootoutStatements.title: A new north–south connection over the A15 should also include a strip planted with trees and shrubs.
The municipality must not cut funding for the Bonkelaarhuis.
The municipality should continue to support the establishment of the asylum seekers center (azc).
